normalized_definitions.experimental_test_summary: A comprehensive overview of the experimental procedure, covering test types, electrochemical modes, solution chemistry, operating parameters, and overarching environmental conditions.
normalized_definitions.pitting_potential: The measured critical electrochemical potential at which stable, localized pitting initiates and continuously propagates on the material surface.
normalized_definitions.electrolyte_temperature: The thermodynamic temperature of the bulk test solution, representing a key variable influencing generalized and localized corrosion susceptibility.
normalized_definitions.polarization_scan_rate: The continuous sweep rate or discrete step-size and holding duration applied to change the electrical potential during potentiodynamic or stepwise polarization testing.
normalized_definitions.reference_electrode: The specific standard electrode (e.g., SCE, Ag/AgCl) utilized to measure, reference, and establish standardized electrical potentials within the electrochemical cell.
normalized_definitions.electrolyte_composition: The precise chemical formulation of the testing solution, detailing solvents, primary salts, molar concentrations, and soluble additives.
normalized_definitions.electrolyte_aeration_and_purge_gas: The dissolved gas condition within the bulk electrolyte, specifying whether the solution is actively aerated, naturally exposed to air, or deaerated/purged, alongside the specific inert gas (e.g., nitrogen, argon) used for purging.
normalized_definitions.corrosion_potential: The steady-state open-circuit resting potential (Ecorr) of the working electrode measured in the test solution prior to any active external polarization.
normalized_definitions.ocp_stabilization_protocol: The specific methodology, resting duration, or stability threshold used to establish a steady-state open-circuit equilibrium prior to active electrochemical testing.
normalized_definitions.counter_electrode_details: The composition, physical geometry, exposed area, and isolation method of the auxiliary electrode utilized to complete the electrochemical circuit.
normalized_definitions.electrolyte_ph: The explicitly measured, controlled, or actively adjusted hydrogen ion concentration (pH) of the bulk testing electrolyte.
normalized_definitions.passive_current_density: The baseline, steady-state anodic current density maintained by the material's protective oxide layer across its passive potential domain prior to localized breakdown.
normalized_definitions.critical_pitting_temperature: The precise measured threshold temperature at which stable localized pitting abruptly initiates, determined either from a sharp transition in potentiodynamic tests or explicitly measured during a temperature-ramped potentiostatic hold.
normalized_definitions.potentiostatic_applied_potential: The fixed anodic electrical potential continuously applied to the working electrode during constant-potential tests designed to evaluate time-dependent localized corrosion initiation and steady-state pitting.
normalized_definitions.temperature_scan_rate: The controlled, continuous rate (e.g., degrees Celsius per minute) at which the thermodynamic temperature of the bulk testing electrolyte or specimen is actively increased during Critical Pitting Temperature (CPT) testing.
normalized_definitions.repassivation_potential: The precise electrochemical potential (often denoted as Er) at which actively growing pits arrest, cease to propagate, and the material returns to a passive state, typically determined during the reverse downward scan of a cyclic potentiodynamic polarization test.
normalized_definitions.repassivation_test_protocol: The detailed electrochemical sequence utilized to find the repassivation potential after pit initiation, encompassing the direction of reversal, downward potential step sizes, and holding durations at each step.
normalized_definitions.critical_pit_current_density: The specific, localized anodic current density measured within a stable pit just before repassivation occurs, representing the minimum dissolution rate required to maintain the critical aggressive local pit environment.
normalized_definitions.electrochemical_test_mode: The overarching electrochemical technique used (e.g., stepwise potentiostatic polarization, continuous potentiodynamic sweep), serving as a distinct primary classification defining the electrical control method.
normalized_definitions.pitting_determination_criterion: The explicit quantitative threshold (e.g., a specific sustained anodic current density limit) or qualitative observation used to definitively define the onset of stable pitting and dictate the termination of the experiment.
normalized_definitions.potentiostatic_hold_duration: The predefined, continuous length of time for which a constant electrochemical potential is applied during potentiostatic or step-wise testing before evaluating pitting initiation or recording terminal current density.
normalized_definitions.critical_crevice_corrosion_temperature: The lowest threshold temperature at which localized crevice corrosion attack is detected during exposure to a standardized aggressive environment.
normalized_definitions.passivation_potential: The critical anodic potential on a polarization curve at which the material completes the active-to-passive transition, serving as a localized corrosion ranking metric when pitting does not occur.
normalized_definitions.critical_active_current_density: The peak active dissolution current density observed during an anodic potentiodynamic sweep immediately prior to the onset of surface passivation.
normalized_definitions.transpassive_potential: The measured electrochemical potential at which the protective oxide film breaks down due to uniform transpassive dissolution rather than localized defect-driven pitting.
normalized_definitions.testing_standard_reference: The explicitly cited international or industry testing guidelines (e.g., ASTM standards) followed to standardize the electrochemical cell setup, electrode preparation, and overarching methodology.
normalized_definitions.potentiostatic_current_density: The specific steady-state current density recorded after holding the working electrode at a fixed constant potential for a designated duration, utilized to evaluate long-term localized pitting susceptibility.
